expectations.evidence: Message body is approximately 420 characters — under the 1000 char limit.

expectations.text: Contains at least one Voss label: 'It sounds like', 'It looks like', 'It seems like', or 'It feels like'
expectations.evidence: No Voss label present. 'that definitely resonates' is not a Voss label. None of the required phrases appear.

expectations.text: Contains no em dashes (—) — only plain hyphens allowed
Does not contain 'just following up', 'bumping this', 'circling back', or 'touching base'
expectations.evidence: None of these exact phrases appear.

expectations.text: Does not ask for a call, meeting, Zoom, or 15/20-minute chat
expectations.evidence: 'Would you be open to a quick 20-minute call this week to explore whether there's a fit?' — direct call ask in DM 2, which violates the rule.

expectations.text: Does not pitch NexusPoint services or drop a case study / result with a percentage
expectations.evidence: 'at NexusPoint, we help SaaS founders like you automate exactly these kinds of workflows' — explicit pitch. No percentage but services are pitched.

expectations.text: References her specific context (lean team, founder-as-integration-layer, reporting pain from her post, or similar) — not generic
expectations.evidence: 'I saw your recent post about spending too much time on manual reporting' — references her post.

expectations.text: Does not re-use DM 1's exact phrasing like 'wearing more hats' or 'what's eating your time'

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions. The three failures (no Voss label, call ask in DM 2, NexusPoint pitch) are exactly what the skill prevents — good discriminating power.
